WITH one AS (
    SELECT date_trunc('year', date) as timeOne, COUNT(*) as trans_count
    FROM trans
    GROUP BY date_trunc('year', date)
),
two AS (
    SELECT date_trunc('year', date) as timeTwo, COUNT(*) as loan_count
    FROM loan
    GROUP BY date_trunc('year', date)
)
SELECT 
    COALESCE(timeOne, timeTwo) as year,
    COALESCE(trans_count, 0) + COALESCE(loan_count, 0) as count
FROM one
FULL OUTER JOIN two ON timeOne = timeTwo
ORDER BY year

-- Create the custom function
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION what_quarter_is(date_in DATE, start_month INTEGER DEFAULT 1)
RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
BEGIN
    RETURN FLOOR(
        (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM date_in) - start_month + 12) % 12 / 3.0
    )::INTEGER + 1;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql IMMUTABLE;

    amount
FROM trans

WITH RECURSIVE path_cte AS (
  -- Base case: Start from cards that have no previous card (head of chains)
  SELECT 
    c.id, 
    c.nextCardId, 
    c.id::TEXT AS Path,
    c.id AS start_id
  FROM collection c
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM collection c2 
    WHERE c2.nextCardId = c.id
  )
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive case: Follow the nextCardId chain forward
  SELECT 
    c.id, 
    c.nextCardId, 
    cte.Path || ' --> ' || c.id::TEXT,
    cte.start_id
  FROM collection c
  JOIN path_cte cte ON cte.nextCardId = c.id
)
SELECT Path
FROM path_cte
WHERE nextCardId IS NULL  -- Only show complete paths (ending at tail nodes)
ORDER BY start_id

WITH ranked_banks AS (
    SELECT 
        trans_id,
        account_id,
        date,
        bank,
        CASE 
            WHEN bank IS NOT NULL THEN 
                ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY account_id ORDER BY date, trans_id)
        END AS bank_group
    FROM trans
),
filled_banks AS (
    SELECT 
        trans_id,
        account_id,
        date,
        bank,
        MAX(bank_group) OVER (
            PARTITION BY account_id 
            ORDER BY date, trans_id 
            ROWS BETWEEN CURRENT ROW AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
        ) AS next_bank_group
    FROM ranked_banks
)
SELECT 
    fb.trans_id,
    fb.account_id,
    fb.date,
    fb.bank,
    rb.bank AS next_bank
FROM filled_banks fb
LEFT JOIN ranked_banks rb 
    ON fb.account_id = rb.account_id 
    AND fb.next_bank_group = rb.bank_group

WITH monthly_purchases AS (
    -- Convert purchase_date to proper date format and get distinct user-month combinations
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        user_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_DATE(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD'))::DATE AS month_date
    FROM purchase
),
streak_groups AS (
    -- Identify consecutive month groups using the difference between row number and month sequence
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        month_date,
        month_date - (ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY month_date) || ' months')::INTERVAL AS streak_group
    FROM monthly_purchases
),
streak_lengths AS (
    -- Calculate the length of each streak for each user
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        streak_group,
        COUNT(*) AS streak_length
    FROM streak_groups
    GROUP BY user_id, streak_group
),
longest_streaks AS (
    -- Get the longest streak for each user
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        MAX(streak_length) AS longest_streak
    FROM streak_lengths
    GROUP BY user_id
)
-- Count how many users have each longest streak length
SELECT 
    longest_streak AS streak_length,
    COUNT(*) AS num_users
FROM longest_streaks
GROUP BY longest_streak
ORDER BY streak_length

SELECT 
    base_table.relname as base_table,
    base_attr.attname as base_col,
    ref_table.relname as referencing_table,
    ref_attr.attname as referencing_col,
    pg_get_constraintdef(c.oid) as constraint_sql
FROM pg_constraint c
JOIN pg_class base_table ON c.confrelid = base_table.oid
JOIN pg_class ref_table ON c.conrelid = ref_table.oid
JOIN LATERAL unnest(c.confkey, c.conkey) WITH ORDINALITY AS keys(base_attnum, ref_attnum, ord) ON true
JOIN pg_attribute base_attr ON base_attr.attrelid = c.confrelid AND base_attr.attnum = keys.base_attnum
JOIN pg_attribute ref_attr ON ref_attr.attrelid = c.conrelid AND ref_attr.attnum = keys.ref_attnum
WHERE base_table.relname = 'users'
  AND c.contype = 'f'
  AND c.confrelid != c.conrelid
ORDER BY referencing_table, referencing_col

WITH LastTransactionPerStation AS (
  -- Get the last transaction at each gas station for each customer
  SELECT
    *,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid 
      ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC, transactionid DESC
    ) AS rn_per_station
  FROM transactions_1k
),
FilteredTransactions AS (
  -- Keep only the last transaction per gas station
  SELECT *
  FROM LastTransactionPerStation
  WHERE rn_per_station = 1
),
CustomerIssues AS (
  -- Identify if customer has any amount < 10 issues and find the first one chronologically
  SELECT
    customerid,
    MIN(CASE 
      WHEN amount < 10 THEN date || ' ' || time || ' ' || LPAD(transactionid::text, 20, '0')
    END) AS first_issue_key
  FROM FilteredTransactions
  GROUP BY customerid
),
FinalSelection AS (
  SELECT
    ft.*,
    ci.first_issue_key,
    date || ' ' || time || ' ' || LPAD(transactionid::text, 20, '0') AS current_key,
    -- For customers without issues, find their last transaction overall
    MAX(date || ' ' || time || ' ' || LPAD(transactionid::text, 20, '0')) 
      OVER (PARTITION BY ft.customerid) AS last_transaction_key
  FROM FilteredTransactions ft
  LEFT JOIN CustomerIssues ci ON ft.customerid = ci.customerid
)
SELECT
  customerid,
  transactionid,
  gasstationid,
  amount,
  date,
  time,
  price
FROM FinalSelection
WHERE
  -- If customer has an issue (amount < 10), show the first occurrence
  (first_issue_key IS NOT NULL AND current_key = first_issue_key)
  OR
  -- If customer has no issues, show the last transaction
  (first_issue_key IS NULL AND current_key = last_transaction_key)
ORDER BY customerid, date, time

WITH qr1(item, subgroup) AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup1 
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
), 
qr2(item, subgroup) AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup2 
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
),
combined AS (
    SELECT qr1.item, qr1.subgroup as subgroup1, qr2.subgroup as subgroup2 
    FROM qr1 
    FULL OUTER JOIN qr2 ON qr1.item = qr2.item
),
-- Create edges between items that share subgroups
edges AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT c1.item as item1, c2.item as item2
    FROM combined c1
    JOIN combined c2 ON (c1.subgroup1 = c2.subgroup1 OR c1.subgroup2 = c2.subgroup2)
    WHERE c1.item < c2.item
),
-- Find connected components using recursive CTE
components AS (
    SELECT item, item as group_id
    FROM combined
    
    UNION
    
    SELECT c.item, LEAST(c.group_id, e.item2) as group_id
    FROM components c
    JOIN edges e ON c.item = e.item1
    
    UNION
    
    SELECT c.item, LEAST(c.group_id, e.item1) as group_id
    FROM components c
    JOIN edges e ON c.item = e.item2
)
SELECT 
    c.item, 
    c.subgroup1, 
    c.subgroup2,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY MIN(comp.group_id)) as group_id
FROM combined c
JOIN components comp ON c.item = comp.item
GROUP BY c.item, c.subgroup1, c.subgroup2
ORDER BY group_id, c.item

    SELECT

group_markers AS (
    SELECT

-- Create the state transition function for forward fill
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION GapFillInternal(s anyelement, v anyelement) 
RETURNS anyelement AS $$ 
BEGIN 
    IF v IS NOT NULL THEN 
        RETURN v;
    ELSIF s IS NOT NULL THEN 
        RETURN s;
    ELSE 
        RETURN NULL;
    END IF;
END; 
$$ LANGUAGE PLPGSQL;

-- Create the aggregate function
CREATE AGGREGATE GapFill(anyelement) (
    SFUNC = GapFillInternal, 
    STYPE = anyelement
);

WITH parent_posts AS (
  SELECT 
    p1.id as parent_id,
    p1.acceptedanswerid as child_id,
    p1.score as parent_score,
    p2.score as child_score
  FROM posts p1
  LEFT JOIN posts p2 ON p1.acceptedanswerid = p2.id
  WHERE p1.acceptedanswerid IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT DISTINCT p.id, p.acceptedanswerid, p.posttypeid, p.score
FROM posts p
WHERE 
  -- Include parent posts with score >= 20
  (p.id IN (SELECT parent_id FROM parent_posts WHERE parent_score >= 20))
  OR
  -- Include child posts where parent score < 20 but child score >= 20
  (p.id IN (SELECT child_id FROM parent_posts WHERE parent_score < 20 AND child_score >= 20))
  OR
  -- Include posts with score >= 20 that are not involved in any parent-child relationship
  (p.score >= 20 
   AND p.id NOT IN (SELECT parent_id FROM parent_posts WHERE parent_id IS NOT NULL)
   AND p.id NOT IN (SELECT child_id FROM parent_posts WHERE child_id IS NOT NULL))
ORDER BY p.id

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to() 
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$ 
BEGIN 
    UPDATE team_attributes ta
    SET eff_to = subquery.next_date
    FROM ( 
        SELECT 
            id,
            COALESCE( 
                LEAD(TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE, 1) OVER ( 
                    PARTITION BY team_api_id 
                    ORDER BY TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE ASC
                ), 
                TO_DATE('5999-12-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD') 
            ) - INTERVAL '1 day' AS next_date
        FROM team_attributes
        WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
    ) AS subquery
    WHERE ta.id = subquery.id; 
    
    RETURN NEW; 
END; 
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER after_insert_team_attributes 
AFTER INSERT ON team_attributes 
FOR EACH ROW 
EXECUTE PROCEDURE update_team_attributes_eff_to()

WITH RECURSIVE EmpMgrCTE AS (
    -- Base case: Start with all employees and their direct managers
    SELECT 
        e.id as employeeId,
        e.salary as employeeSalary,
        1 as hierarchical_level,
        e.bossid as bossId,
        m.salary as bossSalary,
        CASE WHEN m.salary >= 2 * e.salary THEN 1 ELSE 0 END as found_qualifying_boss
    FROM staff e
    LEFT JOIN staff m ON e.bossid = m.id
    WHERE e.bossid IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: Move up the hierarchy until we find a qualifying boss
    SELECT 
        cte.employeeId,
        cte.employeeSalary,
        cte.hierarchical_level + 1,
        m.bossid as bossId,
        mb.salary as bossSalary,
        CASE WHEN mb.salary >= 2 * cte.employeeSalary THEN 1 ELSE 0 END as found_qualifying_boss
    FROM EmpMgrCTE cte
    INNER JOIN staff m ON cte.bossId = m.id
    LEFT JOIN staff mb ON m.bossid = mb.id
    WHERE cte.found_qualifying_boss = 0  -- Only continue if we haven't found a qualifying boss yet
      AND m.bossid IS NOT NULL  -- Stop if there's no higher manager
)
SELECT DISTINCT ON (employeeId)
    employeeId,
    employeeSalary,
    hierarchical_level,
    bossId,
    bossSalary
FROM EmpMgrCTE
WHERE found_qualifying_boss = 1  -- Only return rows where we found a qualifying boss
ORDER BY employeeId, hierarchical_level  -- Get the lowest level (first occurrence) for each employee

WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (
    -- Base case: start with the first row
    SELECT 
        id, 
        amount, 
        amount AS cum_sum, 
        1 AS bucket
    FROM transfer 
    WHERE id = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: process subsequent rows
    SELECT 
        t.id,
        t.amount,
        CASE 
            WHEN cte.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN t.amount
            ELSE cte.cum_sum + t.amount 
        END AS cum_sum,
        CASE 
            WHEN cte.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN cte.bucket + 1
            ELSE cte.bucket 
        END AS bucket
    FROM cte 
    JOIN transfer t ON t.id = cte.id + 1
)
SELECT 
    id, 
    amount,
    cum_sum, 
    bucket
FROM cte
ORDER BY id

    AVG("C") AS c 
FROM (
    SELECT 
        floor(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM ("TIMESTAMP" - '2021-01-07T00:00:00.000Z'::timestamp)) / (7 * 24 * 60 * 60)) AS interval_number,
        "CL",
        "C"
    FROM "Experiment" 
    WHERE "TIMESTAMP" >= '2021-01-07T00:00:00.000Z' 
        AND "TIMESTAMP" <= '2022-01-06T23:59:59.999Z'
) subquery
GROUP BY interval_number 
ORDER BY interval_number ASC

WITH hero_attribute(hero_id, attribute_id, attribute_value) AS (
    VALUES (1, 1, 80), (2, 1, 75), (3, 1, 95), (4, 1, 85), (5, 1, 90), (6, 1, 70)
),
base_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_value,
        ((hero_id - 1) / 2) AS base_group
    FROM hero_attribute
),
expanded_groups AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        bg1.base_group AS group_id,
        bg2.hero_id,
        bg2.attribute_value
    FROM base_groups bg1
    CROSS JOIN base_groups bg2
    WHERE bg2.hero_id BETWEEN (bg1.base_group * 2 + 1 - 2) AND (bg1.base_group * 2 + 2 + 2)
)

FROM expanded_groups
GROUP BY group_id
ORDER BY group_id

WITH sorted_circuits AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    -- Sort and concatenate circuit names for group A
    TRIM(
      CASE 
        WHEN circui_name_a1 <= COALESCE(circui_name_a2, '~') AND circui_name_a1 <= COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '~') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_a1, '')
        WHEN circui_name_a2 <= COALESCE(circui_name_a1, '~') AND circui_name_a2 <= COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '~') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_a2, '')
        ELSE COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '')
      END || ' ' ||
      CASE 
        WHEN (circui_name_a1 > COALESCE(circui_name_a2, '~') OR circui_name_a1 > COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '~')) 
          AND (circui_name_a1 < COALESCE(circui_name_a2, '~') OR circui_name_a1 < COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '~'))
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_a1, '')
        WHEN (circui_name_a2 > COALESCE(circui_name_a1, '~') OR circui_name_a2 > COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '~')) 
          AND (circui_name_a2 < COALESCE(circui_name_a1, '~') OR circui_name_a2 < COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '~'))
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_a2, '')
        ELSE COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '')
      END || ' ' ||
      CASE 
        WHEN circui_name_a1 >= COALESCE(circui_name_a2, '') AND circui_name_a1 >= COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_a1, '')
        WHEN circui_name_a2 >= COALESCE(circui_name_a1, '') AND circui_name_a2 >= COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_a2, '')
        ELSE COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '')
      END
    ) AS a,
    -- Sort and concatenate circuit names for group B
    TRIM(
      CASE 
        WHEN circui_name_b1 <= COALESCE(circui_name_b2, '~') AND circui_name_b1 <= COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '~') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_b1, '')
        WHEN circui_name_b2 <= COALESCE(circui_name_b1, '~') AND circui_name_b2 <= COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '~') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_b2, '')
        ELSE COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '')
      END || ' ' ||
      CASE 
        WHEN (circui_name_b1 > COALESCE(circui_name_b2, '~') OR circui_name_b1 > COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '~')) 
          AND (circui_name_b1 < COALESCE(circui_name_b2, '~') OR circui_name_b1 < COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '~'))
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_b1, '')
        WHEN (circui_name_b2 > COALESCE(circui_name_b1, '~') OR circui_name_b2 > COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '~')) 
          AND (circui_name_b2 < COALESCE(circui_name_b1, '~') OR circui_name_b2 < COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '~'))
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_b2, '')
        ELSE COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '')
      END || ' ' ||
      CASE 
        WHEN circui_name_b1 >= COALESCE(circui_name_b2, '') AND circui_name_b1 >= COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_b1, '')
        WHEN circui_name_b2 >= COALESCE(circui_name_b1, '') AND circui_name_b2 >= COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_b2, '')
        ELSE COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '')
      END
    ) AS b
  FROM race_info
)
SELECT 
  a,
  b,
  CASE WHEN a = b THEN 'MATCH' ELSE 'DIFFERENT' END AS comparison
FROM sorted_circuits

WITH date_series AS (
    SELECT dd::date AS day_start
    FROM generate_series('2022-01-01'::timestamp, '2022-01-31'::timestamp, '1 day'::interval) dd
),
converted_incidents AS (
    SELECT 
        to_timestamp(incident_start / 1000) AS incident_start,
        to_timestamp(incident_end / 1000) AS incident_end
    FROM race_incidents
    WHERE incident_start IS NOT NULL AND incident_end IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ds.day_start,
    COALESCE(SUM(
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (
            LEAST(ci.incident_end, (ds.day_start + INTERVAL '1 day')::timestamp) -
            GREATEST(ci.incident_start, ds.day_start::timestamp)
        )) / 60
    ), 0) AS incident_minutes
FROM date_series ds
LEFT JOIN converted_incidents ci 
    ON ci.incident_start < (ds.day_start + INTERVAL '1 day')::timestamp
    AND ci.incident_end > ds.day_start::timestamp
GROUP BY ds.day_start
ORDER BY ds.day_start

WITH update_result AS (
    UPDATE loan
    SET status = 'updated_status',
        date = 'new_date',
        amount = 'new_amount'
    WHERE loan_id = 'loan_id_value'
    AND status = 'current_status'
    RETURNING loan_id, status
),
loan_check AS (
    SELECT 
        loan_id,
        status as current_status
    FROM loan
    WHERE loan_id = 'loan_id_value'
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM update_result) THEN 'successful_update'
        WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM loan_check WHERE current_status != 'current_status') THEN 'version_conflict'
        WHEN NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM loan_check) THEN 'loan_not_found'
        ELSE 'unknown'
    END as outcome,
    COALESCE((SELECT loan_id FROM update_result), (SELECT loan_id FROM loan_check)) as loan_id

WITH RECURSIVE calculated_results AS (
    -- Base case: first row (ordered by id)
    SELECT 
        id,
        trx,
        event,
        result_good,
        ROUND((trx * event)::numeric, 6) AS result,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY id) AS rn
    FROM sales_transactions
    WHERE id = (SELECT MIN(id) FROM sales_transactions)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: each subsequent row
    SELECT 
        st.id,
        st.trx,
        st.event,
        st.result_good,
        ROUND(((cr.result + st.trx) * st.event)::numeric, 6) AS result,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY st.id) AS rn
    FROM sales_transactions st
    INNER JOIN calculated_results cr ON st.id > cr.id
    WHERE st.id = (
        SELECT MIN(id) 
        FROM sales_transactions 
        WHERE id > cr.id
    )
)
SELECT 
    id,
    result
FROM calculated_results
ORDER BY id

WITH daily_prices AS (
    SELECT 
        card_id,
        DATE(date_value) as trading_day,
        date_value,
        price,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY card_id, DATE(date_value) ORDER BY date_value ASC) as rn_first,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY card_id, DATE(date_value) ORDER BY date_value DESC) as rn_last_within_15min
    FROM card_prices
    WHERE EXTRACT(HOUR FROM date_value) * 60 + EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM date_value) <= 
          (EXTRACT(HOUR FROM (SELECT MIN(date_value) FROM card_prices cp2 WHERE cp2.card_id = card_prices.card_id AND DATE(cp2.date_value) = DATE(card_prices.date_value))) * 60 + 
           EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM (SELECT MIN(date_value) FROM card_prices cp2 WHERE cp2.card_id = card_prices.card_id AND DATE(cp2.date_value) = DATE(card_prices.date_value)))) + 15
),
first_prices AS (
    SELECT card_id, trading_day, price as first_price
    FROM daily_prices
    WHERE rn_first = 1
),
last_prices_15min AS (
    SELECT 
        card_id, 
        trading_day, 
        price as last_price_15min
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            card_id,
            DATE(date_value) as trading_day,
            date_value,
            price,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY card_id, DATE(date_value) ORDER BY date_value DESC) as rn
        FROM card_prices cp
        WHERE date_value <= (
            SELECT MIN(date_value) + INTERVAL '15 minutes'
            FROM card_prices cp2
            WHERE cp2.card_id = cp.card_id 
            AND DATE(cp2.date_value) = DATE(cp.date_value)
        )
    ) sub
    WHERE rn = 1
),
daily_differences AS (
    SELECT 
        fp.card_id,
        fp.trading_day,
        lp.last_price_15min - fp.first_price as price_difference
    FROM first_prices fp
    INNER JOIN last_prices_15min lp 
        ON fp.card_id = lp.card_id 
        AND fp.trading_day = lp.trading_day
)
SELECT 
    card_id,
    AVG(price_difference) as avg_difference
FROM daily_differences
GROUP BY card_id
ORDER BY card_id

WITH datapoint_variables AS (
    SELECT 
        dv.datapoint_id,
        MAX(CASE WHEN v.name = 'ProductType' THEN vv.name END) AS product_type,
        MAX(CASE WHEN v.name = 'publishing_language' THEN vv.name END) AS language,
        MAX(CASE WHEN v.name = 'genre' THEN vv.name END) AS genre
    FROM datapoint_variablevalue dv
    JOIN variablevalue vv ON dv.variablevalue_id = vv.id
    JOIN variable v ON vv.variable_id = v.id
    GROUP BY dv.datapoint_id
),
all_combinations AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        td.years,
        sd.name AS country,
        vv1.name AS product_type,
        vv2.name AS language,
        vv3.name AS genre
    FROM time_dimension td
    CROSS JOIN spatial_dimension sd
    CROSS JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT name FROM variablevalue WHERE variable_id = 1) vv1
    CROSS JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT name FROM variablevalue WHERE variable_id = 2) vv2
    CROSS JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT name FROM variablevalue WHERE variable_id = 3) vv3
)
SELECT 
    ac.years,
    ac.country,
    ac.product_type,
    ac.language,
    ac.genre,
    COALESCE(SUM(dp.value), 0) AS total_sales
FROM all_combinations ac
LEFT JOIN time_dimension td ON ac.years = td.years
LEFT JOIN spatial_dimension sd ON ac.country = sd.name
LEFT JOIN datapoints dp ON dp.time_dimension_id = td.id AND dp.spatial_dimension_id = sd.id
LEFT JOIN datapoint_variables dv ON dp.id = dv.datapoint_id 
    AND ac.product_type = dv.product_type 
    AND ac.language = dv.language 
    AND ac.genre = dv.genre
GROUP BY ac.years, ac.country, ac.product_type, ac.language, ac.genre
ORDER BY ac.years DESC, ac.country, ac.product_type, ac.language, ac.genre

WITH RECURSIVE weekdays AS (
    SELECT generate_series(0, 60) AS offset
),
dates AS (
    SELECT 
        CURRENT_DATE + (w.offset || ' days')::interval AS date,
        EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM CURRENT_DATE + (w.offset || ' days')::interval) AS day_of_week
    FROM weekdays w
    WHERE EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM CURRENT_DATE + (w.offset || ' days')::interval) IN (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7)
),
schedule_blocks_with_dates AS (
    SELECT 
        sb.*,
        d.date AS block_date
    FROM schedule_blocks sb
    JOIN dates d ON TRIM(sb.day_of_week) ILIKE TRIM(to_char(d.date, 'Day'))
    WHERE sb.is_available = TRUE
        AND d.date >= CURRENT_DATE
        AND d.date <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '2 months'
)
SELECT 
    block_id,
    user_id,
    block_date AS date,
    start_time,
    end_time
FROM schedule_blocks_with_dates
ORDER BY date, start_time

WITH RECURSIVE date_range AS (
    SELECT 
        TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36' AS start_ts,
        TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24' AS end_ts,
        DATE(TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36') AS current_date,
        DATE(TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24') AS end_date
),
weekday_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        start_ts,
        end_ts,
        -- Calculate full weekdays between dates
        (SELECT COUNT(*) 
         FROM generate_series(
             DATE(start_ts) + INTERVAL '1 day',
             DATE(end_ts) - INTERVAL '1 day',
             INTERVAL '1 day'
         ) AS d
         WHERE EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM d) < 6
        ) AS full_weekdays,
        -- Handle start date
        CASE 
            WHEN EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM start_ts) < 6 THEN 
                (TIMESTAMP '23:59:59' - start_ts::TIME)
            ELSE INTERVAL '0'
        END AS start_day_time,
        -- Handle end date
        CASE 
            WHEN EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM end_ts) < 6 THEN 
                end_ts::TIME - TIMESTAMP '00:00:00'
            ELSE INTERVAL '0'
        END AS end_day_time
    FROM date_range
),
total_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        (full_weekdays * INTERVAL '1 day') + start_day_time + end_day_time AS total_interval
    FROM weekday_calc
)
SELECT 
    EXTRACT(DAY FROM total_interval) || ' days ' ||
    EXTRACT(HOUR FROM total_interval) || ' hours ' ||
    EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM total_interval) || ' minutes ' ||
    FLOOR(EXTRACT(SECOND FROM total_interval)) || ' seconds' AS time_diff
FROM total_calc

WITH RECURSIVE item_costs AS (
    -- Base case: Direct resource costs and immediate item costs
    SELECT 
        item_id,
        item_price,
        uses_item_id,
        uses_item_price,
        resource_id,
        resource_price,
        quantity,
        0 as depth
    FROM store
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: Expand nested item dependencies
    SELECT 
        ic.item_id,
        ic.item_price,
        s.uses_item_id,
        s.uses_item_price,
        s.resource_id,
        s.resource_price,
        ic.quantity * s.quantity as quantity,
        ic.depth + 1
    FROM item_costs ic
    JOIN store s ON ic.uses_item_id = s.item_id
    WHERE ic.uses_item_id IS NOT NULL
      AND ic.depth < 10  -- Prevent infinite recursion
)
SELECT 
    s.item_id,
    s.item_price as retail_price,
    -- Partial craft price: only direct resources and items (no recursion)
    SUM(DISTINCT CASE 
        WHEN s.resource_id IS NOT NULL THEN s.resource_price * s.quantity 
        ELSE 0 
    END) + 
    SUM(DISTINCT CASE 
        WHEN s.uses_item_id IS NOT NULL THEN s.uses_item_price * s.quantity 
        ELSE 0 
    END) as partial_craft_price,
    -- Full craft price: recursively calculated costs
    COALESCE(
        (SELECT SUM(resource_price * quantity) 
         FROM item_costs 
         WHERE item_costs.item_id = s.item_id 
           AND resource_id IS NOT NULL), 0
    ) as full_craft_price
FROM store s
GROUP BY s.item_id, s.item_price
ORDER BY s.item_id

WITH RECURSIVE
-- Get buy transactions with running totals
buys AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_id,
        molecule_id,
        date,
        molecules,
        usd_total,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) AS cumulative_bought,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) - molecules AS prev_cumulative_bought
    FROM transaction
    WHERE sell = 0
),
-- Get sell transactions with running totals
sells AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_id,
        molecule_id,
        date,
        molecules,
        usd_total,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) AS cumulative_sold,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) - molecules AS prev_cumulative_sold
    FROM transaction
    WHERE sell = 1
),
-- Match buys and sells
matched AS (
    SELECT 
        b.molecule_id,
        b.date AS buy_date,
        s.date AS sell_date,
        b.transaction_id AS buy_transaction_id,
        s.transaction_id AS sell_transaction_id,
        -- Calculate overlap between this buy and this sell
        CASE 
            WHEN b.cumulative_bought <= s.prev_cumulative_sold THEN 0
            WHEN b.prev_cumulative_bought >= s.cumulative_sold THEN 0
            ELSE LEAST(b.cumulative_bought, s.cumulative_sold) - GREATEST(b.prev_cumulative_bought, s.prev_cumulative_sold)
        END AS molecules,
        b.usd_total / b.molecules AS buy_price_per_molecule,
        s.usd_total / s.molecules AS sell_price_per_molecule
    FROM buys b
    CROSS JOIN sells s
    WHERE b.molecule_id = s.molecule_id
)
SELECT 
    molecule_id,
    buy_date,
    sell_date,
    molecules,
    ROUND(molecules * buy_price_per_molecule, 2) AS buy_usd_total,
    ROUND(molecules * sell_price_per_molecule, 2) AS sell_usd_total
FROM matched
WHERE molecules > 0
ORDER BY molecule_id, sell_date, buy_date

WITH split_events AS (
  SELECT string_to_array(event_description, '#') AS tokens
  FROM match_events
),
min_length AS (
  SELECT MIN(array_length(tokens, 1)) AS min_len
  FROM split_events
),
position_check AS (
  SELECT 
    generate_series(1, (SELECT min_len FROM min_length)) AS pos
),
common_prefix_length AS (
  SELECT 
    COALESCE(
      (SELECT MIN(pos) - 1 
       FROM position_check 
       WHERE (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT tokens[pos]) FROM split_events) > 1),
      (SELECT min_len FROM min_length)
    ) AS prefix_len
)
SELECT 
  array_to_string(
    (string_to_array((SELECT MIN(event_description) FROM match_events), '#'))[1:(SELECT prefix_len FROM common_prefix_length)],
    '#'
  ) AS common_prefix

WITH RankedPrices AS (
    SELECT 
        product_id,
        price,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY product_id ORDER BY effective_from DESC) as rn
    FROM ProductPrice
)
SELECT DISTINCT
    p.product_id,
    p.product_name
FROM Product p
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM RankedPrices curr
    JOIN RankedPrices prev ON curr.product_id = prev.product_id AND prev.rn = 2
    WHERE curr.product_id = p.product_id
        AND curr.rn = 1
        AND curr.price < prev.price
)
ORDER BY p.product_id

WITH user_groups AS (
  -- First, find all connected users through shared refs
  SELECT DISTINCT
    u1.id as user_id,
    COALESCE(u2.ref1, u1.ref1, u2.ref2, u1.ref2) as group_ref
  FROM users u1
  LEFT JOIN users u2 ON u1.ref1 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref1 = u2.ref2 
                     OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref2
),
merged_users AS (
  -- Group users by their shared reference
  SELECT 
    MIN(group_ref) as group_id,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT user_id) as ids
  FROM user_groups
  GROUP BY group_ref
),
asset_ownership AS (
  -- Get assets with their owners, including orphaned assets
  SELECT 
    a.id as asset_id,
    a.owner,
    u.id as user_id
  FROM assets a
  LEFT JOIN users u ON a.owner = u.ref1 OR a.owner = u.ref2
)
SELECT 
  COALESCE(mu.ids, ARRAY[]::integer[]) as ids,
  ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.username) FILTER (WHERE u.username IS NOT NULL) as usernames,
  ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.ref1) FILTER (WHERE u.ref1 IS NOT NULL) as refs1,
  ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT COALESCE(u.ref2, ao.owner)) as refs2,
  COUNT(DISTINCT ao.asset_id) as asset_count,
  ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ao.asset_id ORDER BY ao.asset_id) as asset_ids
FROM asset_ownership ao
LEFT JOIN merged_users mu ON ao.user_id = ANY(mu.ids)
LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = ANY(COALESCE(mu.ids, ARRAY[ao.user_id]))
GROUP BY mu.group_id, ao.owner, mu.ids
ORDER BY MIN(ao.asset_id)

SELECT 
    CD.customer_id,
    COALESCE(S.total_sales, 0) AS total_sales,
    COALESCE(S.total_amount, 0) AS total_amount,
    COALESCE(C.total_communications, 0) AS total_communications,
    COALESCE(F.total_feedback, 0) AS total_feedback,
    COALESCE(F.total_positive_feedback, 0) AS total_positive_feedback,
    COALESCE(F.total_negative_feedback, 0) AS total_negative_feedback

        SUM(amount) AS total_amount

    GROUP BY customer_id
) S ON CD.customer_id = S.customer_id

    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        COUNT(*) AS total_communications
    FROM communications
    GROUP BY customer_id
) C ON CD.customer_id = C.customer_id
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        COUNT(*) AS total_feedback,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE feedback_type = 'positive') AS total_positive_feedback,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE feedback_type = 'negative') AS total_negative_feedback
    FROM feedback
    GROUP BY customer_id
) F ON CD.customer_id = F.customer_id

WITH RECURSIVE A(GroupNum, n, RollingSum, id, rn) AS (
    -- Base case: get the first row by prediction_code
    SELECT 
        1 AS GroupNum,
        Performance_Prediction AS n,
        Performance_Prediction AS RollingSum,
        prediction_code AS id,
        1 AS rn
    FROM performance_predictions
    WHERE prediction_code = (SELECT MIN(prediction_code) FROM performance_predictions)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: process subsequent rows
    SELECT
        CASE
            WHEN A.RollingSum + P.Performance_Prediction > 200
            THEN A.GroupNum + 1
            ELSE A.GroupNum
        END AS GroupNum,
        P.Performance_Prediction AS n,
        CASE
            WHEN A.RollingSum + P.Performance_Prediction > 200
            THEN P.Performance_Prediction
            ELSE A.RollingSum + P.Performance_Prediction
        END AS RollingSum,
        P.prediction_code AS id,
        A.rn + 1 AS rn
    FROM A
    JOIN performance_predictions AS P ON P.prediction_code = (
        SELECT MIN(pp.prediction_code)
        FROM performance_predictions pp
        WHERE pp.prediction_code > A.id
    )
)
SELECT
    GroupNum,
    MAX(RollingSum) AS group_sum
FROM A
GROUP BY GroupNum
ORDER BY GroupNum

-- Recursive CTE to find all dependent curricula
WITH RECURSIVE curricula_to_upgrade AS (
  -- Base case: Start with the target curriculum
  SELECT DISTINCT c.curriculum_id
  FROM curricula c
  WHERE c.curriculum_id = 1  -- "Introduction to Programming"
    AND c.status = 'installed'

  UNION

  -- Recursive case: Find curricula that share resources with already-identified curricula
  SELECT DISTINCT c2.curriculum_id
  FROM curricula_to_upgrade ctu
  JOIN curriculum_resources cr1 ON ctu.curriculum_id = cr1.curriculum_id
  JOIN curriculum_resources cr2 ON cr1.resource_key = cr2.resource_key
  JOIN curricula c2 ON cr2.curriculum_id = c2.curriculum_id
  WHERE c2.status = 'installed'
    AND c2.curriculum_id NOT IN (SELECT curriculum_id FROM curricula_to_upgrade)
)
UPDATE curricula
SET status = 'to upgrade'
WHERE curriculum_id IN (
    SELECT curriculum_id
    FROM curricula_to_upgrade
)

        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 
             AND MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 THEN 'Open'
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 THEN 'Open'
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 THEN 'Planning'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
    END AS consolidated_status,
    CASE 
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 
            THEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN remaining END)
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 
             AND MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 
            THEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN spent END) + 
                 MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN spent END)
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 
            THEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN spent END)
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 
            THEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN spent END)

WITH set_has_reprint AS (
    -- Identify which sets contain at least one reprint
    SELECT DISTINCT setcode
    FROM cards
    WHERE isreprint = 1
),

    -- Get the highest card ID for each set
    SELECT setcode, MAX(id) as max_id
    FROM cards
    GROUP BY setcode
)
SELECT c.*
FROM cards c
WHERE 
    -- Condition 1: All cards from sets that contain at least one reprint
    c.setcode IN (SELECT setcode FROM set_has_reprint)
    OR
    -- Condition 2: Only the highest ID card from sets with no reprints
    (c.setcode NOT IN (SELECT setcode FROM set_has_reprint)
     AND c.id IN (SELECT max_id FROM max_id_per_set WHERE setcode = c.setcode))
ORDER BY c.setcode, c.id

WITH zero_consumption AS (
    -- Get only records with zero consumption
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        date,
        consumption,
        -- Convert YYYYMM text to a sortable format and calculate row number
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customerid ORDER BY date) as rn,
        -- Calculate difference between date rank and overall rank
        -- This creates groups for consecutive periods
        TO_DATE(date, 'YYYYMM') as date_parsed
    FROM yearmonth
    WHERE consumption = 0
),
island_groups AS (
    -- Create island identifiers for consecutive periods
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        date,
        consumption,
        date_parsed,
        -- Subtract row number from month sequence to identify islands
        -- Consecutive months will have the same group identifier
        EXTRACT(YEAR FROM date_parsed) * 12 + EXTRACT(MONTH FROM date_parsed) - rn as island_group
    FROM zero_consumption
)
SELECT 
    customerid,
    MIN(date) as period_start,
    MAX(date) as period_end,
    COUNT(*) as months_in_period
FROM island_groups
GROUP BY customerid, island_group
ORDER BY customerid, period_start

WITH TeamRecords AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        team_api_id,
        date,
        buildupplayspeed,
        buildupplaypassing,
        defenceteamwidth,
        chancecreationshooting,
        -- Get next record's values for the same team
        LEAD(buildupplayspeed) OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id ORDER BY date) AS next_buildupplayspeed,
        LEAD(buildupplaypassing) OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id ORDER BY date) AS next_buildupplaypassing,
        -- Get previous record's values for the same team
        LAG(defenceteamwidth) OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id ORDER BY date) AS prev_defenceteamwidth,
        LAG(chancecreationshooting) OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id ORDER BY date) AS prev_chancecreationshooting,
        -- Count total records per team
        COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id) AS team_record_count
    FROM team_attributes
)
SELECT
    id,
    team_api_id,
    date,
    buildupplayspeed AS current_buildupplayspeed,
    next_buildupplayspeed,
    buildupplaypassing AS current_buildupplaypassing,
    next_buildupplaypassing,
    defenceteamwidth AS current_defenceteamwidth,
    prev_defenceteamwidth,
    chancecreationshooting AS current_chancecreationshooting,
    prev_chancecreationshooting
FROM TeamRecords
WHERE 
    team_record_count > 1  -- Only teams with more than one record
    AND prev_defenceteamwidth > buildupplayspeed  -- Previous defensive width > current build-up speed
    AND prev_defenceteamwidth IS NOT NULL  -- Ensure we have a previous record
ORDER BY team_api_id, date

        BUILDUPPLAYSPEED AS VALUE, 
        'Team_Attributes' AS IDENTIFIER, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY BUILDUPPLAYSPEED DESC NULLS LAST) AS RN 
    FROM team_attributes
) 
SELECT 
    PLAYERATTRS.VALUE AS player_overall_rating, 
    PLAYERATTRS.IDENTIFIER AS player_identifier, 
    TEAMATTRS.VALUE AS team_buildupspeed, 
    TEAMATTRS.IDENTIFIER AS team_identifier,
    PLAYERATTRS.RN AS rank
FROM (
    SELECT 
        OVERALL_RATING AS VALUE, 
        'Player_Attributes' AS IDENTIFIER, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY OVERALL_RATING DESC NULLS LAST) AS RN 
    FROM player_attributes
) AS PLAYERATTRS 
FULL OUTER JOIN TEAMATTRS ON PLAYERATTRS.RN = TEAMATTRS.RN
ORDER BY COALESCE(PLAYERATTRS.RN, TEAMATTRS.RN)

SELECT

    c.name AS circuit_name,
    d.forename || ' ' || d.surname AS driver_name,

    res.position AS finish_position,

INNER JOIN races r ON res.raceid = r.raceid
INNER JOIN circuits c ON r.circuitid = c.circuitid

WITH t AS
  (SELECT "date",
          "match_api_id" AS code,
          CURRENT_DATE - "date"::date AS duration,
          COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY "date"::date, "match_api_id", CURRENT_DATE - "date"::date) AS cnt,
          MIN(CURRENT_DATE - "date"::date) OVER (PARTITION BY "date"::date, "match_api_id") AS min_dur
   FROM "match")
SELECT t."date",
       t.code,
       t.duration,
       CASE
           WHEN t.min_dur = 0
                OR t.cnt > 1 THEN 'Y'
           ELSE 'N'
       END AS flg
FROM t
WHERE t."date"::date = '2008-11-22'

WITH MoleculesWithMultipleBondTypes AS (
    SELECT molecule_id
    FROM bond
    GROUP BY molecule_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT bond_type) > 1
)
SELECT DISTINCT 
    b.molecule_id, 
    MIN(b.bond_type) as bond_type
FROM bond b
INNER JOIN MoleculesWithMultipleBondTypes m 
    ON b.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
GROUP BY b.molecule_id
ORDER BY b.molecule_id

WITH pages AS (
    SELECT 1 AS page_id, 1 AS power_id 
    UNION ALL SELECT 1, 7 
    UNION ALL SELECT 2, 1 
    UNION ALL SELECT 3, 1 
    UNION ALL SELECT 3, 7 
    UNION ALL SELECT 3, 89 
    UNION ALL SELECT 4, 20 
    UNION ALL SELECT 5, 55 
    UNION ALL SELECT 5, 1 
    UNION ALL SELECT 6, 1 
    UNION ALL SELECT 6, 13
), 
items AS (
    SELECT 10 AS hero_id, 1 AS power_id 
    UNION ALL SELECT 10, 7 
    UNION ALL SELECT 10, 20 
    UNION ALL SELECT 12, 1 
    UNION ALL SELECT 12, 55
),
page_power_counts AS (
    SELECT page_id, COUNT(*) AS total_powers
    FROM pages
    GROUP BY page_id
)
SELECT p.page_id, i.hero_id
FROM pages AS p
INNER JOIN items AS i ON p.power_id = i.power_id
INNER JOIN page_power_counts AS ppc ON p.page_id = ppc.page_id
GROUP BY p.page_id, i.hero_id, ppc.total_powers
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT i.power_id) = ppc.total_powers

WITH status_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        driverid, 
        statusid, 
        COUNT(*) AS status_count
    FROM results
    GROUP BY driverid, statusid
),
ranked_statuses AS (
    SELECT 
        driverid,
        statusid,
        status_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY driverid ORDER BY status_count DESC, statusid) AS rn
    FROM status_counts
)
SELECT 
    d.driverid,
    d.forename,
    d.surname,
    rs.statusid,
    s.status,
    rs.status_count
FROM ranked_statuses rs
JOIN drivers d ON rs.driverid = d.driverid
JOIN status s ON rs.statusid = s.statusid
WHERE rs.rn = 1
ORDER BY d.surname, d.forename

WITH home_matches AS (
    -- Get all home matches with win/loss/draw indicator
    SELECT 
        home_team_api_id AS team_api_id,
        date,
        CASE 
            WHEN home_team_goal > away_team_goal THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS is_win,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY home_team_api_id ORDER BY date) AS match_number
    FROM match
    WHERE date IS NOT NULL
),
wins_with_groups AS (
    -- Create groups for consecutive wins using gap-and-island technique
    SELECT 
        team_api_id,
        date,
        is_win,
        match_number,
        -- Subtract row number of wins from overall row number to create groups
        match_number - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY team_api_id, is_win 
            ORDER BY date
        ) AS streak_group
    FROM home_matches
),
winning_streaks AS (
    -- Filter only wins and aggregate by streak
    SELECT 
        team_api_id,
        MIN(date) AS start_date,
        MAX(date) AS end_date,
        COUNT(*) AS match_count,
        streak_group
    FROM wins_with_groups
    WHERE is_win = 1
    GROUP BY team_api_id, streak_group
    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1  -- You can change this to filter minimum streak length
)
SELECT 
    team_api_id,
    start_date,
    end_date,
    match_count
FROM winning_streaks
ORDER BY team_api_id, start_date

-- Method 1: Using unnest and string_to_array to split and convert to rows
SELECT 
    unnest(string_to_array('1.25,5.34,6.9', ','))::FLOAT as decimal_value;

-- Method 2: If you need the values as separate columns (for a fixed number of values)
SELECT 
    split_part('1.25,5.34,6.9', ',', 1)::FLOAT as value1,
    split_part('1.25,5.34,6.9', ',', 2)::FLOAT as value2,
    split_part('1.25,5.34,6.9', ',', 3)::FLOAT as value3;

-- Method 3: More flexible approach with row numbers (recommended for variable length)
SELECT 
    row_number() OVER () as position,
    value::FLOAT as decimal_value
FROM unnest(string_to_array('1.25,5.34,6.9', ',')) as value;

-- Method 4: If you have this in a table column and want to process it
-- Example: Extracting decimal values from a column
SELECT 
    t.trans_id,
    unnest(string_to_array(some_column, ','))::FLOAT as extracted_value
FROM trans t
WHERE some_column IS NOT NULL

WITH PatientExamData AS (
    SELECT 
        P.ID AS PATIENTID, 
        P.SEX, 
        P.BIRTHDAY, 
        E."Examination Date", 
        E.DIAGNOSIS, 
        E.THROMBOSIS, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY P.ID ORDER BY E."Examination Date" NULLS FIRST) AS ExamRank 
    FROM patient AS P 
    LEFT JOIN examination AS E ON P.ID = E.ID
), 
LatestLabResults AS (
    SELECT 
        L.ID, 
        L.PLT, 
        L.WBC, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY L.ID ORDER BY L."date" DESC NULLS LAST) AS LabRank 
    FROM laboratory AS L 
    WHERE L."date" <= CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
), 
PatientRiskProfile AS (
    SELECT 
        PED.PATIENTID, 
        PED.SEX, 
        PED.BIRTHDAY, 
        PED."Examination Date", 
        PED.DIAGNOSIS, 
        PED.THROMBOSIS, 
        CASE 
            WHEN LLR.PLT < 150 OR LLR.WBC > 11 THEN 'High' 
            WHEN PED.THROMBOSIS IS NULL THEN 'Unknown' 
            ELSE 'Normal' 
        END AS RiskCategory 
    FROM patientexamdata AS PED 
    LEFT JOIN latestlabresults AS LLR ON PED.PATIENTID = LLR.ID AND LLR.LabRank = 1
) 
SELECT 
    PATIENTID, 
    SEX, 
    BIRTHDAY, 
    COUNT("Examination Date") AS EXAMINATIONCOUNT, 
    SUM(CASE WHEN THROMBOSIS = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS THROMBOSISCOUNT, 
    SUM(CASE WHEN THROMBOSIS = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS NOTHROMBOSISCOUNT, 
    MAX(CASE WHEN RiskCategory = 'High' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS HighRiskFlag 
FROM patientriskprofile 
GROUP BY PATIENTID, SEX, BIRTHDAY 
HAVING COUNT("Examination Date") > 0 
ORDER BY SUM(CASE WHEN THROMBOSIS = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) DESC NULLS LAST, PATIENTID NULLS FIRST

WITH RaceResults AS (
    SELECT
        r.raceId,
        r.year,
        r.date,
        res.driverId,
        res.points,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY res.driverId ORDER BY r.date, r.raceId) AS CareerRaceNumber
    FROM results res
    JOIN races r ON res.raceId = r.raceId
),
DriverFirstFiveRaces AS (
    SELECT
        driverId,
        points,
        CareerRaceNumber
    FROM RaceResults
    WHERE CareerRaceNumber <= 5
)
SELECT
    d.driverId,
    d.forename || ' ' || d.surname AS DriverName,
    MAX(CASE WHEN CareerRaceNumber = 1 THEN points END) AS Race1_Points,
    MAX(CASE WHEN CareerRaceNumber = 2 THEN points END) AS Race2_Points,
    MAX(CASE WHEN CareerRaceNumber = 3 THEN points END) AS Race3_Points,
    MAX(CASE WHEN CareerRaceNumber = 4 THEN points END) AS Race4_Points,
    MAX(CASE WHEN CareerRaceNumber = 5 THEN points END) AS Race5_Points
FROM DriverFirstFiveRaces r
JOIN drivers d ON r.driverId = d.driverId
GROUP BY d.driverId, d.forename, d.surname
ORDER BY d.surname, d.forename

WITH years AS (
    -- Get all distinct years from the bond_list table
    SELECT DISTINCT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM transact_dt_tm) AS year
    FROM bond_list
    WHERE transact_dt_tm IS NOT NULL
),
all_bond_types AS (
    -- Get all bond types from the reference table
    SELECT bond_type, bond_seq
    FROM bond_ref
),
year_bond_cross AS (
    -- Create a cross join to get all combinations of years and bond types
    SELECT 
        y.year,
        b.bond_type,
        b.bond_seq
    FROM years y
    CROSS JOIN all_bond_types b
),
actual_counts AS (
    -- Get actual counts of bonds by year and type
    SELECT 
        EXTRACT(YEAR FROM transact_dt_tm) AS year,
        bond_type,
        COUNT(*) AS bond_count
    FROM bond_list
    WHERE transact_dt_tm IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM transact_dt_tm), bond_type
)
SELECT 
    ybc.year,
    ybc.bond_type,
    COALESCE(ac.bond_count, 0) AS bond_count
FROM year_bond_cross ybc
LEFT JOIN actual_counts ac 
    ON ybc.year = ac.year 
    AND ybc.bond_type = ac.bond_type
ORDER BY 
    ybc.year,
    ybc.bond_seq

    EXTRACT(epoch FROM 
        (LEAD(T.date || ' ' || T.time, 1) OVER (ORDER BY T.date, T.time))::TIMESTAMP 
        - (T.date || ' ' || T.time)::TIMESTAMP
    )::BIGINT AS TimeDifference
FROM transactions_1k AS T

WITH t AS
  (SELECT e.*,
          ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY e.id
                             ORDER BY e."Examination Date") AS seqnum
   FROM examination AS e),
     cte AS
  (SELECT t.id,
          t."Examination Date" AS startdate,
          t.seqnum,
          'N' AS isrehire,
          t."Examination Date" AS anchordate
   FROM t
   WHERE t.seqnum = 1
   UNION ALL SELECT t.id,
                    t."Examination Date" AS startdate,
                    t.seqnum,
                    CASE
                        WHEN t."Examination Date" > (cte.anchordate + INTERVAL '1 year') THEN 'Y'
                        ELSE 'N'
                    END AS isrehire,
                    CASE
                        WHEN t."Examination Date" > (cte.anchordate + INTERVAL '1 year') THEN t."Examination Date"
                        ELSE cte.anchordate
                    END AS anchordate
   FROM cte
   INNER JOIN t ON cte.id = t.id 
                AND t.seqnum = cte.seqnum + 1)
SELECT id,
       startdate,
       seqnum,
       isrehire
FROM cte
ORDER BY id,
         startdate

WITH RECURSIVE ancestral_path AS (
  -- Base case: start with sets that have a parent
  SELECT 
    s.id,
    s.code,
    s.parentcode,
    s.code AS path
  FROM sets AS s
  WHERE s.parentcode IS NOT NULL
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive case: join with parent sets and build the path
  SELECT 
    ap.id,
    ap.code,
    s.parentcode,
    ap.path || '-' || s.code AS path
  FROM ancestral_path AS ap
  JOIN sets AS s ON ap.parentcode = s.code
  WHERE s.code IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
  id,
  code,
  path AS ancestralCode
FROM (
  SELECT 
    id,
    code,
    path,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY id ORDER BY LENGTH(path) DESC) AS rn
  FROM ancestral_path
) ranked
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY id

WITH hero_activity AS
  (SELECT id,
          activity_date,
          activity_value,
          hero_id
   FROM (
         VALUES (1,
                 '2023-01-01',
                 10,
                 1), (2,
                      '2023-01-02',
                      20,
                      1), (3,
                           '2023-02-01',
                           15,
                           1), (4,
                                '2023-02-02',
                                25,
                                1), (5,
                                     '2023-01-03',
                                     30,
                                     2), (6,
                                          '2023-02-03',
                                          40,
                                          2)) AS t (id, activity_date, activity_value, hero_id)),
     monthly_totals AS
  (SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM activity_date) AS year,
          EXTRACT(MONTH FROM activity_date) AS month,
          SUM(activity_value) AS month_total
   FROM hero_activity
   GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM activity_date),
            EXTRACT(MONTH FROM activity_date)),
     activity_totals AS
  (SELECT ha.id,
          ha.activity_date,
          ha.activity_value,
          SUM(ha.activity_value) OVER (
                                       ORDER BY ha.activity_date) AS run_tot,
                                      SUM(ha.activity_value) OVER (PARTITION BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM ha.activity_date),
                                                                               EXTRACT(MONTH FROM ha.activity_date)
                                                                   ORDER BY ha.activity_date) AS m_run_tot,
                                                                  EXTRACT(YEAR FROM ha.activity_date) AS curr_year,
                                                                  EXTRACT(MONTH FROM ha.activity_date) AS curr_month
   FROM hero_activity ha)
SELECT at.id,
       at.activity_date,
       at.activity_value,
       at.run_tot,
       at.m_run_tot,
       COALESCE(mt.month_total, 0) AS prev_month_total
FROM activity_totals at
LEFT JOIN monthly_totals mt ON mt.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM (at.activity_date - INTERVAL '1 month'))
AND mt.month = EXTRACT(MONTH FROM (at.activity_date - INTERVAL '1 month'))
ORDER BY at.activity_date

WITH match_dates AS (
    SELECT m.country_id,
           m.date,
           m.league_id,
           m.season,
           DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY m.country_id ORDER BY m.date DESC) AS match_rank
    FROM match m
    WHERE m.date IS NOT NULL
),
country_stats AS (
    SELECT c.id AS country_id,
           c.name AS country_name,
           COUNT(DISTINCT m.league_id) AS league_count,
           COUNT(DISTINCT m.season) AS season_count
    FROM country c
    LEFT JOIN match m ON c.id = m.country_id
    GROUP BY c.id, c.name
),
match_summary AS (
    SELECT md.country_id,
           md.date,
           md.match_rank,
           string_agg(DISTINCT l.name, ', ' ORDER BY l.name) AS leagues_played
    FROM match_dates md
    LEFT JOIN league l ON md.league_id = l.id
    WHERE md.match_rank <= 2
    GROUP BY md.country_id, md.date, md.match_rank
)
SELECT cs.country_id,
       cs.country_name,
       MAX(CASE WHEN ms.match_rank = 1 THEN ms.date END) AS last_match_date,
       MAX(CASE WHEN ms.match_rank = 1 THEN ms.leagues_played END) AS last_match_leagues,
       MAX(CASE WHEN ms.match_rank = 2 THEN ms.date END) AS prev_match_date,
       MAX(CASE WHEN ms.match_rank = 2 THEN ms.leagues_played END) AS prev_match_leagues,
       cs.league_count,
       cs.season_count
FROM country_stats cs
LEFT JOIN match_summary ms ON cs.country_id = ms.country_id
GROUP BY cs.country_id, cs.country_name, cs.league_count, cs.season_count
HAVING MAX(CASE WHEN ms.match_rank = 1 THEN ms.date END) IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY MAX(CASE WHEN ms.match_rank = 1 THEN ms.date END) DESC

SELECT 
    a1.atom_id AS atom_id1, 
    a2.atom_id AS atom_id2, 
    b.bond_id, 
    b.bond_type 
FROM atom AS a1 
CROSS JOIN atom AS a2 
LEFT JOIN connected AS c 
    ON a1.atom_id = c.atom_id AND a2.atom_id = c.atom_id2
LEFT JOIN bond AS b 
    ON c.bond_id = b.bond_id 
WHERE a1.molecule_id = a2.molecule_id 
ORDER BY a1.atom_id NULLS FIRST, a2.atom_id NULLS FIRST

WITH ChangeHistory AS (
    SELECT
        s.cdscode,
        s.school,
        s.fundingtype,
        s.lastupdate,
        LAG(s.school) OVER (PARTITION BY s.cdscode ORDER BY s.lastupdate) AS PreviousSchool,
        LAG(s.fundingtype) OVER (PARTITION BY s.cdscode ORDER BY s.lastupdate) AS PreviousFundingType
    FROM schools s
)

SELECT
    cdscode,
    'School' AS ColumnName,
    COALESCE(PreviousSchool, '') AS OldValue,
    COALESCE(school, '') AS NewValue
FROM ChangeHistory
WHERE school IS DISTINCT FROM PreviousSchool
  AND PreviousSchool IS NOT NULL

UNION ALL

SELECT
    cdscode,
    'FundingType' AS ColumnName,
    COALESCE(PreviousFundingType, '') AS OldValue,
    COALESCE(fundingtype, '') AS NewValue
FROM ChangeHistory
WHERE fundingtype IS DISTINCT FROM PreviousFundingType
  AND PreviousFundingType IS NOT NULL

ORDER BY
    cdscode,
    CASE WHEN ColumnName = 'School' THEN 1 ELSE 2 END

WITH RECURSIVE T_period_futur AS
  (SELECT 1 AS Tpf_period_id,
          CAST('2021-10-25' AS DATE) AS Tpf_start_period
   UNION ALL SELECT Tpf_period_id + 1 AS Tpf_period_id,
                    Tpf_start_period + INTERVAL '1 week'
   FROM T_period_futur
   WHERE Tpf_period_id < 12 )
SELECT Tpf_period_id,
       Tpf_start_period,
       EXTRACT(YEAR FROM Tpf_start_period) AS Tpf_year,
       EXTRACT(ISOYEAR FROM Tpf_start_period)::TEXT || '.' || LPAD(EXTRACT(WEEK FROM Tpf_start_period)::TEXT, 2, '0') AS Tpf_period_number,
       Tpf_start_period + INTERVAL '6 days' AS Tpf_end_period
FROM T_period_futur

WITH cte AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    "Examination Date",
    thrombosis,
    LAG(thrombosis, 1) OVER (PARTITION BY id ORDER BY "Examination Date") AS risklag,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY id, 
                   EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "Examination Date"),
                   EXTRACT(MONTH FROM "Examination Date")
      ORDER BY "Examination Date" DESC
    ) AS rn
  FROM examination
)
SELECT 
  id,
  DATE_TRUNC('month', "Examination Date")::DATE AS startofmonth,
  CASE
    WHEN risklag < thrombosis THEN 'Yes'
    ELSE 'No'
  END AS increase_risk,
  CASE
    WHEN risklag > thrombosis THEN 'Yes'
    ELSE 'No'
  END AS decrease_risk
FROM cte
WHERE rn = 1
  AND risklag IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY id, startofmonth
SELECT info FROM _json_update_result

SELECT 
    EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')) AS year,
    EXTRACT(WEEK FROM TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')) AS week_number,
    SUM(CASE WHEN home_team_goal > away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS home_team_wins,
    SUM(CASE WHEN home_team_goal < away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS away_team_wins,
    SUM(CASE WHEN home_team_goal = away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS draws,
    COUNT(*) AS total_matches
FROM match
WHERE date IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 
    EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')),
    EXTRACT(WEEK FROM TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS'))
ORDER BY year, week_number

SELECT
  json_object_agg(
    'country_id' || m.country_id,
    match_details
  ) AS "JSON"
FROM (
  SELECT
    country_id,
    json_agg(
      json_build_object(

WITH RECURSIVE ExpSequence AS (
    -- Get all experiments using material 2, ordered by exp_id
    SELECT
        exp_id,
        mid,
        qty,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY exp_id) AS rn
    FROM experiment
    WHERE mid = 2
),
StockCheck AS (
    -- Base case: first experiment
    SELECT
        e.exp_id,
        e.mid,
        e.qty,
        i.qty AS available_stock,
        CASE WHEN e.qty <= i.qty THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS executable,
        CASE WHEN e.qty <= i.qty THEN i.qty - e.qty ELSE i.qty END AS remaining_stock,
        e.rn
    FROM ExpSequence e
    CROSS JOIN inventory i
    WHERE e.rn = 1 AND i.mid = 2
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: subsequent experiments
    SELECT
        e.exp_id,
        e.mid,
        e.qty,
        s.remaining_stock AS available_stock,
        CASE WHEN e.qty <= s.remaining_stock THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS executable,
        CASE WHEN e.qty <= s.remaining_stock 
             THEN s.remaining_stock - e.qty 
             ELSE s.remaining_stock 
        END AS remaining_stock,
        e.rn
    FROM ExpSequence e
    INNER JOIN StockCheck s ON e.rn = s.rn + 1
)
SELECT
    exp_id,
    mid,
    executable
FROM StockCheck
ORDER BY exp_id

WITH PaymentRanks AS (

        DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY payments DESC NULLS LAST) AS dense_rank_num
    FROM loan
    WHERE payments IS NOT NULL
),
DistinctPayments AS (
    SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT payments) AS distinct_payment_count 
    FROM loan
    WHERE payments IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT distinct_payment_count FROM DistinctPayments) < 2 THEN NULL
        ELSE (SELECT payments FROM PaymentRanks WHERE dense_rank_num = 2 LIMIT 1)
    END AS SecondHighest

WITH
    set_a (molecule_id, element_count) AS (
        SELECT molecule_id, COUNT(DISTINCT element)
        FROM atom
        GROUP BY molecule_id
        HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT element) > 1
    ),
    set_b (molecule_id, element_count) AS (
        SELECT m.molecule_id, COUNT(DISTINCT a.element)
        FROM molecule m
        JOIN atom a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
        WHERE m.label IS NOT NULL
        GROUP BY m.molecule_id
    ),
    unique_a (molecule_id, element_count, rn, density_rank) AS (
        SELECT
            molecule_id,
            element_count,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY element_count) AS rn,
            DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY element_count DESC) AS density_rank
        FROM set_a
    ),
    unique_b (molecule_id, element_count, rn, density_rank) AS (
        SELECT
            molecule_id,
            element_count,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY element_count) AS rn,
            DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY element_count DESC) AS density_rank
        FROM set_b
    ),
    -- Filter set A with the complex conditions
    filtered_a AS (
        SELECT ua.*
        FROM unique_a ua
        WHERE 
            -- Check if molecule has the specific bond using EXISTS
            EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM bond b
                WHERE b.molecule_id = ua.molecule_id
                AND b.bond_id = 'TR011_2_24'
            )
            -- Check for connected atoms with same element
            AND EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM connected c
                JOIN atom a1 ON c.atom_id = a1.atom_id
                JOIN atom a2 ON c.atom_id2 = a2.atom_id
                WHERE a1.molecule_id = ua.molecule_id
                AND a1.element = a2.element
            )
    )
SELECT
    COUNT(CASE WHEN fa.molecule_id IS NOT NULL AND ub.molecule_id IS NOT NULL 
               AND fa.rn = ub.rn AND fa.density_rank = ub.density_rank THEN 1 END) AS num_identical_in_a_and_b,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN fa.molecule_id IS NOT NULL AND ub.molecule_id IS NULL THEN 1 END) AS num_not_identical_in_a,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN fa.molecule_id IS NULL AND ub.molecule_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS num_not_identical_in_b
FROM filtered_a fa
FULL OUTER JOIN unique_b ub ON fa.molecule_id = ub.molecule_id AND fa.rn = ub.rn

WITH KEYWORDMATCHES AS
  (SELECT P.ID,
          CASE
              WHEN POSITION(LOWER('Aaron') IN LOWER(P.PLAYER_NAME)) > 0 THEN 'Aaron'
          END AS COLAKEYWORD,
          CASE
              WHEN POSITION(LOWER('Cresswell') IN LOWER(P.PLAYER_NAME)) > 0 THEN 'Cresswell'
          END AS COLBKEYWORD,
          CASE
              WHEN POSITION(LOWER('Doran') IN LOWER(P.PLAYER_NAME)) > 0 THEN 'Doran'
          END AS COLCKEYWORD
   FROM PLAYER AS P),
     KEYWORDSCORES AS
  (SELECT ID,
          STRING_AGG(COLAKEYWORD, ', ') FILTER (WHERE COLAKEYWORD IS NOT NULL) AS KEYWORDSA,
          STRING_AGG(COLBKEYWORD, ', ') FILTER (WHERE COLBKEYWORD IS NOT NULL) AS KEYWORDSB,
          STRING_AGG(COLCKEYWORD, ', ') FILTER (WHERE COLCKEYWORD IS NOT NULL) AS KEYWORDSC,
          SUM(CASE
                  WHEN COLAKEYWORD IS NOT NULL THEN 10
                  ELSE 0
              END) AS COLASCORE,
          SUM(CASE
                  WHEN COLBKEYWORD IS NOT NULL THEN 5
                  ELSE 0
              END) AS COLBSCORE,
          SUM(CASE
                  WHEN COLCKEYWORD IS NOT NULL THEN 0.1
                  ELSE 0
              END) AS COLCSCORE
   FROM KEYWORDMATCHES AS KM
   GROUP BY ID)
SELECT DISTINCT P.ID,
                P.PLAYER_NAME AS DESCRIPTION,
                (KS.COLASCORE + KS.COLBSCORE + KS.COLCSCORE) AS SCORE
FROM PLAYER AS P
INNER JOIN KEYWORDSCORES AS KS ON P.ID = KS.ID
ORDER BY SCORE DESC

WITH monthly_periods AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        rate,
        generate_series(
            DATE_TRUNC('day', LOWER(time_span)),
            COALESCE(UPPER(time_span), NOW()),
            INTERVAL '1 month'
        ) AS period_start
    FROM budgets
),
period_details AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        rate,
        period_start::DATE AS period_start_date,
        (period_start + INTERVAL '1 month')::DATE AS period_end_date,
        LEAST(
            (period_start + INTERVAL '1 month')::DATE,
            COALESCE((SELECT UPPER(time_span)::DATE FROM budgets b WHERE b.id = monthly_periods.id), CURRENT_DATE)
        ) AS actual_end_date
    FROM monthly_periods
),
daily_rates AS (
    SELECT 
        period_start_date,
        actual_end_date,
        rate,
        (actual_end_date - period_start_date) AS days_in_period,
        rate::NUMERIC / NULLIF((actual_end_date - period_start_date), 0) AS daily_rate
    FROM period_details
)
SELECT 
    generate_series(period_start_date, actual_end_date - 1, INTERVAL '1 day')::DATE AS date,
    ROUND(daily_rate, 2) AS daily_rate
FROM daily_rates
ORDER BY date

WITH "my_table" AS (
  SELECT 'A' AS "cto", 1 AS "nif", 'A1' AS "file_", 5 AS "filesize"
  UNION ALL SELECT 'A', 1, 'A2', 3
  UNION ALL SELECT 'A', 2, 'A3', 2
  UNION ALL SELECT 'A', 2, 'A4', 1
  UNION ALL SELECT 'B', 1, 'B1', 4
  UNION ALL SELECT 'B', 1, 'B2', 6
),
ordered_data AS (
  SELECT 
    "cto",
    "nif",
    "file_",
    "filesize",
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY "cto" ORDER BY "file_") AS "rn"
  FROM "my_table"
),
nif_groups AS (
  SELECT 
    *,
    SUM(CASE WHEN "nif" != LAG("nif") OVER (PARTITION BY "cto" ORDER BY "rn") THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
      OVER (PARTITION BY "cto" ORDER BY "rn") AS "nif_group",
    SUM("filesize") OVER (PARTITION BY "cto", "nif" ORDER BY "rn" 
      ROWS BETWEEN CURRENT ROW AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING) AS "remaining_nif_size"
  FROM ordered_data
),
nif_group_totals AS (
  SELECT 
    *,
    SUM("filesize") OVER (PARTITION BY "cto", "nif_group") AS "nif_group_total"
  FROM nif_groups
),
partitions AS (
  SELECT 
    *,
    SUM(CASE 
      WHEN "filesize" + LAG(COALESCE("running_size", 0), 1, 0) 
           OVER (PARTITION BY "cto" ORDER BY "rn") + 
           LEAD("nif_group_total", 1, 0) OVER (PARTITION BY "cto" ORDER BY "rn") > 10
      THEN 1 
      ELSE 0 
    END) OVER (PARTITION BY "cto" ORDER BY "rn") AS "partition_change"
  FROM (
    SELECT 
      *,
      SUM("filesize") OVER (PARTITION BY "cto" ORDER BY "rn" 
        ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS "running_size"
    FROM nif_group_totals
  ) sub
),
final_partitions AS (
  SELECT 
    "cto",
    "nif",
    "file_",
    "filesize",
    "rn",
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY "cto" ORDER BY "partition_change") AS "partition"
  FROM partitions
),
with_subpartitions AS (
  SELECT 
    "cto",
    "nif",
    "file_",
    "partition",
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY "cto", "partition" ORDER BY "rn") AS "subpartition",
    SUM("filesize") OVER (PARTITION BY "cto", "partition") AS "total_size"
  FROM final_partitions
)
SELECT 
  "cto",
  "nif",
  "file_",
  "partition",
  "subpartition",
  "total_size"
FROM with_subpartitions
ORDER BY "cto", "partition", "subpartition"

WITH date_ranges AS (
  -- Define your date ranges here (without duplicating from schools table)
  SELECT TO_DATE('2014-01-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_from,
         TO_DATE('2014-01-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_until
  UNION ALL 
  SELECT TO_DATE('2014-02-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_from,
         TO_DATE('2014-02-28', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS valid_until
),
events AS (
  -- Convert ranges to events: start dates (+1) and end dates (-1)
  SELECT valid_from AS dt, 1 AS type
  FROM date_ranges
  UNION ALL
  SELECT valid_until + INTERVAL '1 day' AS dt, -1 AS type
  FROM date_ranges
),
cumulative_counts AS (
  -- Calculate running count at each event
  SELECT 
    dt AS valid_from,
    LEAD(dt) OVER (ORDER BY dt) AS valid_until,
    SUM(type) OVER (ORDER BY dt ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS count
  FROM events
)
SELECT 
  valid_from,
  valid_until - INTERVAL '1 day' AS valid_until,
  count
FROM cumulative_counts
WHERE count > 0
  AND (valid_until IS NULL OR valid_from < valid_until)
ORDER BY valid_from

WITH RECURSIVE vt AS (
    SELECT 
        t.*, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customerid ORDER BY startdate, transactionid) AS seqnum
    FROM transactions_1k t
),
cte AS (
    -- Base case: first transaction for each customer is always unflagged
    SELECT 
        vt.transactionid, 
        vt.customerid, 
        vt.startdate, 
        vt.enddate,
        vt.enddate AS last_unflagged_enddate,  -- Track end date of last unflagged transaction
        0 AS flag,
        vt.seqnum
    FROM vt
    WHERE seqnum = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: process subsequent transactions
    SELECT 
        vt.transactionid, 
        vt.customerid, 
        vt.startdate, 
        vt.enddate,
        -- Update reference end date only if current transaction will be unflagged
        CASE 
            WHEN vt.startdate <= (cte.last_unflagged_enddate + INTERVAL '90 days') 
            THEN cte.last_unflagged_enddate  -- Keep previous reference if flagging
            ELSE vt.enddate  -- Update to current end date if not flagging
        END AS last_unflagged_enddate,
        -- Flag as 1 if within 90 days of last unflagged transaction's end date
        CASE 
            WHEN vt.startdate <= (cte.last_unflagged_enddate + INTERVAL '90 days') 
            THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS flag,
        vt.seqnum
    FROM cte 
    JOIN vt ON vt.seqnum = cte.seqnum + 1 AND vt.customerid = cte.customerid
)
SELECT 
    transactionid, 
    customerid, 
    startdate, 
    enddate,
    flag
FROM cte
ORDER BY customerid, startdate, transactionid

WITH 
-- Step 1: Merge overlapping blackout periods
blackout_starts AS (
    SELECT start_date, end_date
    FROM blackout_periods s1
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1
        FROM blackout_periods s2
        WHERE s2.start_date < s1.start_date
        AND s1.start_date <= s2.end_date
    )
),
blackout_ends AS (
    SELECT start_date, end_date
    FROM blackout_periods t1
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1
        FROM blackout_periods t2
        WHERE t2.start_date <= t1.end_date
        AND t1.end_date < t2.end_date
    )
),
merged_blackouts AS (
    SELECT s.start_date, MIN(e.end_date) as end_date
    FROM blackout_starts s
    INNER JOIN blackout_ends e
    ON s.start_date <= e.end_date
    GROUP BY s.start_date
),
-- Step 2: Invert blackout periods to get non-blackout periods
non_blackout_periods AS (
    SELECT start_date, end_date
    FROM (
        SELECT (end_date + INTERVAL '1 day')::date AS start_date,
               LEAD((start_date - INTERVAL '1 day')::date, 1, '9999-12-31'::date) 
                   OVER (ORDER BY start_date) AS end_date
        FROM merged_blackouts
        
        UNION ALL
        
        SELECT '1900-01-01'::date AS start_date,
               (MIN(start_date) - INTERVAL '1 day')::date AS end_date
        FROM merged_blackouts
    ) k
    WHERE start_date <= end_date
)
-- Step 3: Intersect active periods with non-blackout periods
SELECT 
    GREATEST(aa.start_date, nbp.start_date) AS start_date,
    LEAST(aa.end_date, nbp.end_date) AS end_date
FROM account_activity aa
INNER JOIN non_blackout_periods nbp
    ON aa.start_date <= nbp.end_date
    AND nbp.start_date <= aa.end_date
WHERE GREATEST(aa.start_date, nbp.start_date) <= LEAST(aa.end_date, nbp.end_date)
ORDER BY start_date

WITH member_event_counts AS (
    -- Get attendance count for each member-event combination
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        COUNT(*) AS attendance_count
    FROM attendance
    GROUP BY link_to_member, link_to_event
),
top_events_per_member AS (
    -- Rank events for each member
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        attendance_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY link_to_member ORDER BY attendance_count DESC, link_to_event) AS event_rank
    FROM member_event_counts
),
top_members_per_event AS (
    -- Rank members for each event
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        attendance_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY link_to_event ORDER BY attendance_count DESC, link_to_member) AS member_rank
    FROM member_event_counts
)
-- Combine results: top 2 events per member OR top 1 member per event
SELECT DISTINCT
    link_to_member,
    link_to_event,
    attendance_count
FROM (
    SELECT link_to_member, link_to_event, attendance_count, 'top_event' AS category
    FROM top_events_per_member
    WHERE event_rank <= 2
    
    UNION
    
    SELECT link_to_member, link_to_event, attendance_count, 'top_member' AS category
    FROM top_members_per_event
    WHERE member_rank = 1
) combined
ORDER BY link_to_member, attendance_count DESC, link_to_event

WITH string1_tab AS (

),
string2_tab AS (
    SELECT UNNEST(STRING_TO_ARRAY('25,36,88,98', ',')) AS value
),
string1_parsed AS (
    SELECT TRIM(value)::INTEGER AS num_value
    FROM string1_tab
),
string2_parsed AS (
    SELECT TRIM(value)::INTEGER AS num_value
    FROM string2_tab
),
common_values AS (
    SELECT s1.num_value
    FROM string1_parsed s1
    JOIN string2_parsed s2 ON s1.num_value = s2.num_value
)
SELECT STRING_AGG(num_value::TEXT, ',' ORDER BY num_value) AS common_values
FROM common_values
WHERE num_value IS NOT NULL

WITH atom_stats AS (
    SELECT a.molecule_id, COUNT(a.atom_id) AS atom_count,
           STRING_AGG(a.element, ',' ORDER BY a.atom_id) AS elements
    FROM atom AS a
    GROUP BY a.molecule_id
),
molecule_connectivity AS (
    SELECT m.molecule_id, m.label,
           COUNT(DISTINCT c.atom_id) AS connected_atoms,
           COUNT(DISTINCT c.bond_id) AS bond_count,
           CASE WHEN m.label = '+' THEN 'Positive'
                WHEN m.label = '-' THEN 'Negative'
                ELSE 'Neutral'
           END AS charge_type
    FROM molecule AS m
    LEFT JOIN atom AS a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
    LEFT JOIN connected AS c ON a.atom_id = c.atom_id
    GROUP BY m.molecule_id, m.label
)
SELECT m.molecule_id, m.label, a.atom_id, a.element, b.bond_id, b.bond_type,
       ast.atom_count, ast.elements,
       mc.connected_atoms, mc.charge_type,
       RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY m.molecule_id ORDER BY a.atom_id) AS atom_rank,
       DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY m.molecule_id ORDER BY b.bond_type) AS bond_type_rank
FROM molecule AS m
INNER JOIN atom AS a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
INNER JOIN connected AS c ON a.atom_id = c.atom_id
INNER JOIN bond AS b ON c.bond_id = b.bond_id
LEFT JOIN atom_stats AS ast ON m.molecule_id = ast.molecule_id
LEFT JOIN molecule_connectivity AS mc ON m.molecule_id = mc.molecule_id
WHERE b.bond_type IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY m.molecule_id, atom_rank

ORDER BY mc.match_year, mc.league_name

    FROM event
    WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY type
),

    SELECT
        e.type,
        CAST(strftime('%Y', e.event_date) AS INTEGER) AS year,
        COUNT(*) AS event_count
    FROM event e
    WHERE e.event_date IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY e.type, CAST(strftime('%Y', e.event_date) AS INTEGER)
)

    COALESCE(recent.event_count, 0) - COALESCE(first.event_count, 0) AS year_difference
FROM type_year_ranges tyr
LEFT JOIN type_year_counts recent 
    ON tyr.type = recent.type AND tyr.most_recent_year = recent.year
LEFT JOIN type_year_counts first 
    ON tyr.type = first.type AND tyr.first_year = first.year
ORDER BY tyr.type

WITH "MY_TABLE" AS
  (SELECT '1111' AS "TECHNICAL_ID",
          'NOTIONALCR' AS "ASSET_TYPE"
   UNION ALL SELECT '1111' AS "TECHNICAL_ID",
                    '50000' AS "ASSET_TYPE"
   UNION ALL SELECT '2222' AS "TECHNICAL_ID",
                    'FWDNOTLCR' AS "ASSET_TYPE"
   UNION ALL SELECT '2222' AS "TECHNICAL_ID",
                    '50000' AS "ASSET_TYPE"
   UNION ALL SELECT '3333' AS "TECHNICAL_ID",
                    '50000' AS "ASSET_TYPE"
   UNION ALL SELECT '3333' AS "TECHNICAL_ID",
                    'DUMMY' AS "ASSET_TYPE"),
     "MAP_RECRF_ASSET_TYPE" AS
  (SELECT 'SW' AS "APPLICATION",
          'NOTIONALCR' AS "ASSET_TYPE",
          1 AS "PRIORITY"
   UNION ALL SELECT 'SW' AS "APPLICATION",
                    'NOTIONALDB' AS "ASSET_TYPE",
                    1 AS "PRIORITY"
   UNION ALL SELECT 'SW' AS "APPLICATION",
                    'FWDNOTLCR' AS "ASSET_TYPE",
                    2 AS "PRIORITY"
   UNION ALL SELECT 'SW' AS "APPLICATION",
                    'FWDNOTLDR' AS "ASSET_TYPE",
                    2 AS "PRIORITY"
   UNION ALL SELECT 'SW' AS "APPLICATION",
                    'SWOFFBALCR' AS "ASSET_TYPE",
                    2 AS "PRIORITY"
   UNION ALL SELECT 'SW' AS "APPLICATION",
                    'SWOFFBALDR' AS "ASSET_TYPE",
                    2 AS "PRIORITY"
   UNION ALL SELECT 'SW' AS "APPLICATION",
                    'SWFWNOTLCR' AS "ASSET_TYPE",
                    2 AS "PRIORITY"
   UNION ALL SELECT 'SW' AS "APPLICATION",
                    'SWFWNOTLDB' AS "ASSET_TYPE",
                    2 AS "PRIORITY"
   UNION ALL SELECT 'SW' AS "APPLICATION",
                    '50000' AS "ASSET_TYPE",
                    3 AS "PRIORITY"),
     "CTE" AS
  (SELECT X."TECHNICAL_ID",
          X."ASSET_TYPE",
          ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY X."TECHNICAL_ID"
                             ORDER BY MRAT."PRIORITY") AS "rn"
   FROM "MY_TABLE" X
   INNER JOIN "MAP_RECRF_ASSET_TYPE" MRAT ON X."ASSET_TYPE" = MRAT."ASSET_TYPE"
   WHERE MRAT."APPLICATION" = 'SW')
SELECT "TECHNICAL_ID",
       "ASSET_TYPE"
FROM "CTE"
WHERE "rn" = 1

WITH RECURSIVE time_series AS (
    -- Generate hourly intervals for each event
    SELECT 
        learner_code,
        resource_key,
        start_timestamp,
        end_timestamp,
        DATE_TRUNC('hour', start_timestamp) AS hour_start,
        DATE_TRUNC('hour', start_timestamp) + INTERVAL '1 hour' AS hour_end
    FROM event_usage
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        learner_code,
        resource_key,
        start_timestamp,
        end_timestamp,
        hour_end AS hour_start,
        hour_end + INTERVAL '1 hour' AS hour_end
    FROM time_series
    WHERE hour_end < end_timestamp
),
working_hours_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        learner_code,
        resource_key,
        CASE 
            -- Check if the hour falls on a weekday (Monday=1 to Friday=5)
            WHEN EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM hour_start) BETWEEN 1 AND 5
            -- Check if the hour is within working hours (9:00-18:00)
            AND EXTRACT(HOUR FROM hour_start) BETWEEN 9 AND 17
            THEN 
                -- Calculate overlap between event and this working hour
                EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (
                    LEAST(GREATEST(hour_end, start_timestamp), end_timestamp) - 
                    GREATEST(LEAST(hour_start, end_timestamp), start_timestamp)
                ))
            ELSE 0
        END AS working_seconds
    FROM time_series
    WHERE hour_start < end_timestamp
)
SELECT 
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    SUM(working_seconds) AS total_working_hours_seconds,
    ROUND(SUM(working_seconds) / 3600.0, 2) AS total_working_hours
FROM working_hours_calc
GROUP BY learner_code, resource_key
ORDER BY learner_code, resource_key

WITH match_dates AS (
  SELECT 
    "id",
    TO_TIMESTAMP("date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') AS match_start_time,
    TO_TIMESTAMP("date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') + INTERVAL '2 hours' AS match_end_time,
    EXTRACT(DOW FROM TO_TIMESTAMP("date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')) AS day_of_week
  FROM "match"
  WHERE "date" IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
  m.id,
  m.match_start_time,
  m.match_end_time,
  m.day_of_week,
  ROUND(
    GREATEST(
      EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (
        LEAST(
          m.match_end_time,
          DATE_TRUNC('day', m.match_start_time) + INTERVAL '17 hours'
        ) - 
        GREATEST(
          m.match_start_time,
          DATE_TRUNC('day', m.match_start_time) + INTERVAL '7 hours 30 minutes'
        )
      )) / 60,
      0
    )
  ) AS match_duration_minutes
FROM match_dates m
WHERE m.day_of_week BETWEEN 1 AND 5  -- Monday (1) to Friday (5)
  AND m.match_start_time < DATE_TRUNC('day', m.match_start_time) + INTERVAL '17 hours'  -- Starts before 5 PM
  AND m.match_end_time > DATE_TRUNC('day', m.match_start_time) + INTERVAL '7 hours 30 minutes'  -- Ends after 7:30 AM

        EXTRACT(HOUR FROM CAST("date" AS TIMESTAMP)) +

    FROM "match"
    WHERE "date" IS NOT NULL

target_times AS (
    SELECT 8 AS target_hour, 'morning' AS time_period

        mt.match_date,

            PARTITION BY mt.match_date, tt.target_hour
            ORDER BY ABS(mt.hour_of_day - tt.target_hour), mt."match_api_id"
        ) AS rn
    FROM match_times mt
    CROSS JOIN target_times tt
)

WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (
    -- Base case: Start with leaf nodes (nodes that have color already set)
    SELECT id, parent_id, color
    FROM superhero2
    WHERE color IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: Process parent nodes based on their children's colors
    SELECT 
        s.id, 
        s.parent_id,
        CASE 
            -- If parent has only one child, take that child's color
            WHEN COUNT(c.id) = 1 THEN MAX(c.color)
            -- If parent has two children with same color, take that color
            WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT c.color) = 1 THEN MAX(c.color)
            -- If parent has two children with different colors, make it gray
            ELSE 'gray'
        END AS color
    FROM superhero2 s
    INNER JOIN cte c ON s.id = c.parent_id
    WHERE s.color IS NULL
    GROUP BY s.id, s.parent_id
)

SET color = cte.color
FROM cte
WHERE superhero2.id = cte.id AND superhero2.color IS NULL
